data.name: This portal is the shared entry point for an OMP instance.
data.value: This portal is the shared entry point for an OMP instance.

data.name: No accessible web app instances are currently registered.
data.value: No accessible web app instances are currently registered.

data.name: The admin area is split between manual core administration and advanced automation. For manual setup, focus on instances, hosts, modules, apps, app instances and artifacts. Template- and deployment-driven rollout lives under Automation.
data.value: The admin area is split between manual core administration and advanced automation. For manual setup, focus on instances, hosts, modules, apps, app instances and artifacts. Template- and deployment-driven rollout lives under Automation.

data.name: These pages are the main workflow when setting up and maintaining an OMP instance without HostAgent automation.
data.value: These pages are the main workflow when setting up and maintaining an OMP instance without HostAgent automation.

data.name: Template topology, deployment assignments and deployment tracking are separated from the main workflow until the HostAgent path is more complete.
data.value: Template topology, deployment assignments and deployment tracking are separated from the main workflow until the HostAgent path is more complete.

data.name: Manage admin permissions and role assignments separately from topology and runtime data.
data.value: Manage admin permissions and role assignments separately from topology and runtime data.

data.name: Neutral reference module for web flows in OMP. The page shows a module overview and links onward to the module's configurations.
data.value: Neutral reference module for web flows in OMP. The page shows a module overview and links onward to the module's configurations.

data.name: Continue to the configuration view to review or update the module's current sample configurations.
data.value: Continue to the configuration view to review or update the module's current sample configurations.

data.name: Configurations for Example Web App Module. These values are used by the example module and can be edited without leaving the module interface.
data.value: Configurations for Example Web App Module. These values are used by the example module and can be edited without leaving the module interface.

data.name: Update comment and JSON content for the selected configuration.
data.value: Update comment and JSON content for the selected configuration.

data.name: Set Host base URL to make this route clickable from the Portal.
data.value: Set Host base URL to make this route clickable from the Portal.

data.name: Use these pages when defining desired topology for automated rollout.
data.value: Use these pages when defining desired topology for automated rollout.

data.name: These pages are related to deployment orchestration and future HostAgent-driven execution.
data.value: These pages are related to deployment orchestration and future HostAgent-driven execution.

data.name: App definitions belong to modules and describe what can be deployed. Routes, artifacts, installation paths and runtime policy belong to app instances instead of app definitions.
data.value: App definitions belong to modules and describe what can be deployed. Routes, artifacts, installation paths and runtime policy belong to app instances instead of app definitions.

data.name: Choose how the platform should think about this definition.
data.value: Choose how the platform should think about this definition.

data.name: Delete this app definition? Related artifacts and app instances must be removed first.
data.value: Delete this app definition? Related artifacts and app instances must be removed first.

data.name: Delete is blocked while artifacts, app instances or template rows still reference the app.
data.value: Delete is blocked while artifacts, app instances or template rows still reference the app.

data.name: App instances are the runtime center of OMP. This page handles host placement, artifact choice, routing and verification policy for a concrete app inside an OMP instance.
data.value: App instances are the runtime center of OMP. This page handles host placement, artifact choice, routing and verification policy for a concrete app inside an OMP instance.

data.name: Start by choosing the module instance. Host and app selections are validated against it.
data.value: Start by choosing the module instance. Host and app selections are validated against it.

data.name: The chosen app must belong to the same module definition as the selected module instance.
data.value: The chosen app must belong to the same module definition as the selected module instance.

data.name: If set, the host must belong to the same instance as the selected module instance.
data.value: If set, the host must belong to the same instance as the selected module instance.

data.name: If set, the artifact must belong to the selected app definition.
data.value: If set, the artifact must belong to the selected app definition.

data.name: Use either an absolute URL such as https://www.google.com/ or a host-root-relative path such as app01, /app01, app01/ or /app01/. Relative values are resolved directly from the selected host root without any extra portal prefix.
data.value: Use either an absolute URL such as https://www.google.com/ or a host-root-relative path such as app01, /app01, app01/ or /app01/. Relative values are resolved directly from the selected host root without any extra portal prefix.

data.name: Optional absolute URL when the app is externally reachable.
data.value: Optional absolute URL when the app is externally reachable.

data.name: Physical install location on host, if relevant.
data.value: Physical install location on host, if relevant.

data.name: Friendly install slot or service name.
data.value: Friendly install slot or service name.

data.name: Module-owned config identifier. Leave empty when not used.
data.value: Module-owned config identifier. Leave empty when not used.

data.name: Controls what the platform expects this runtime instance to do.
data.value: Controls what the platform expects this runtime instance to do.

data.name: Optional runtime identity that is expected to report heartbeat.
data.value: Optional runtime identity that is expected to report heartbeat.

data.name: Delete removes the runtime row. Make sure no external system still relies on this app instance key.
data.value: Delete removes the runtime row. Make sure no external system still relies on this app instance key.

data.name: Relative path inside your artifact repository or release output.
data.value: Relative path inside your artifact repository or release output.

data.name: Optional checksum used for later integrity checks.
data.value: Optional checksum used for later integrity checks.

data.name: Delete this artifact? App instances depending on it must be updated first.
data.value: Delete this artifact? App instances depending on it must be updated first.

data.name: Delete is blocked while app instances still point at this artifact.
data.value: Delete is blocked while app instances still point at this artifact.

data.name: Hosts represent real deployment targets inside an OMP instance. For manual setup this is one of the most important core pages.
data.value: Hosts represent real deployment targets inside an OMP instance. For manual setup this is one of the most important core pages.

data.name: Every host belongs to exactly one OMP instance.
data.value: Every host belongs to exactly one OMP instance.

data.name: Logical host identity used by OMP. Keep it short and stable.
data.value: Logical host identity used by OMP. Keep it short and stable.

data.name: Optional absolute base URL for this host. The Portal uses it when an app instance has a relative RoutePath. Enter only protocol, host and optional port.
data.value: Optional absolute base URL for this host. The Portal uses it when an app instance has a relative RoutePath. Enter only protocol, host and optional port.

data.name: Free text for the observed or intended OS version.
data.value: Free text for the observed or intended OS version.

data.name: Delete this host? App instances using it must be moved or removed first.
data.value: Delete this host? App instances using it must be moved or removed first.

data.name: Host deletion is blocked when app instances or deployments still reference the host.
data.value: Host deletion is blocked when app instances or deployments still reference the host.

data.name: Hosts represent concrete runtime targets within an OMP instance. App instances attach to hosts for actual placement and observation.
data.value: Hosts represent concrete runtime targets within an OMP instance. App instances attach to hosts for actual placement and observation.

data.name: Instances are the top-level OMP environments. Templates are optional for manual administration and mainly belong to future automation.
data.value: Instances are the top-level OMP environments. Templates are optional for manual administration and mainly belong to future automation.

data.name: Stable technical key used in links, config and references.
data.value: Stable technical key used in links, config and references.

data.name: Optional context for operators and maintainers.
data.value: Optional context for operators and maintainers.

data.name: Leave empty for a purely manual instance. Template linkage can be added later.
data.value: Leave empty for a purely manual instance. Template linkage can be added later.

data.name: Delete this instance? This only works when no dependent rows remain.
data.value: Delete this instance? This only works when no dependent rows remain.

data.name: Delete is blocked by SQL if hosts, module instances or other dependent rows still reference this instance.
data.value: Delete is blocked by SQL if hosts, module instances or other dependent rows still reference this instance.

data.name: OMP instances are the top-level environments. Instance templates remain optional and primarily belong to the automation workflow.
data.value: OMP instances are the top-level environments. Instance templates remain optional and primarily belong to the automation workflow.

data.name: Modules are definitions. Keep runtime-specific values out of this page and use module instances or app instances for environment-specific configuration.
data.value: Modules are definitions. Keep runtime-specific values out of this page and use module instances or app instances for environment-specific configuration.

data.name: Stable definition key used by apps and instance templates.
data.value: Stable definition key used by apps and instance templates.

data.name: Pick the closest platform role. This is a definition-level classification.
data.value: Pick the closest platform role. This is a definition-level classification.

data.name: Database schema owned by the module, when applicable.
data.value: Database schema owned by the module, when applicable.

data.name: Delete this module? Related apps and instances must be removed first.
data.value: Delete this module? Related apps and instances must be removed first.

data.name: Deleting a module is only possible when dependent apps, module instances and template rows are already gone.
data.value: Deleting a module is only possible when dependent apps, module instances and template rows are already gone.

data.name: Module definitions are reusable building blocks. They do not describe placement or runtime state.
data.value: Module definitions are reusable building blocks. They do not describe placement or runtime state.

data.name: Module instances bind a module definition to a concrete OMP instance. This is where definitions start becoming operational.
data.value: Module instances bind a module definition to a concrete OMP instance. This is where definitions start becoming operational.

data.name: Choose the module definition this module instance represents.
data.value: Choose the module definition this module instance represents.

data.name: Delete this module instance? Related app instances must be removed first.
data.value: Delete this module instance? Related app instances must be removed first.

data.name: SQL blocks deletion while app instances or template mappings still depend on this row.
data.value: SQL blocks deletion while app instances or template mappings still depend on this row.

data.name: Module instances bind module definitions into a specific OMP instance. This is the operational layer above definitions and below app instances.
data.value: Module instances bind module definitions into a specific OMP instance. This is the operational layer above definitions and below app instances.

data.name: Host templates describe reusable host-level desired state for template-driven rollout.
data.value: Host templates describe reusable host-level desired state for template-driven rollout.

data.name: Instance templates belong to the future automation path. They describe desired topology rather than the manually managed live runtime.
data.value: Instance templates belong to the future automation path. They describe desired topology rather than the manually managed live runtime.

data.name: Manage roles, permissions and principal assignments from Portal instead of editing the database directly.
data.value: Manage roles, permissions and principal assignments from Portal instead of editing the database directly.

data.name: Named access bundles that can carry multiple permissions and principals.
data.value: Named access bundles that can carry multiple permissions and principals.

data.name: Low-level capabilities granted to apps and portal areas.
data.value: Low-level capabilities granted to apps and portal areas.

data.name: Create roles, update descriptions, assign permissions and attach principals.
data.value: Create roles, update descriptions, assign permissions and attach principals.

data.name: Create permissions once and reuse them across roles and example modules.
data.value: Create permissions once and reuse them across roles and example modules.

data.name: Permissions are low-level capability names. Keep them stable and descriptive so roles remain understandable.
data.value: Permissions are low-level capability names. Keep them stable and descriptive so roles remain understandable.

data.name: No permissions have been created yet.
data.value: No permissions have been created yet.

data.name: Short explanation of who should receive this role.
data.value: Short explanation of who should receive this role.

data.name: Bind users, groups or service identities to the role. Prefer groups where possible.
data.value: Bind users, groups or service identities to the role. Prefer groups where possible.

data.name: Delete this role? Linked role assignments will also be removed.
data.value: Delete this role? Linked role assignments will also be removed.

data.name: Deleting a role removes all principal and permission links for that role.
data.value: Deleting a role removes all principal and permission links for that role.

data.name: Roles bundle permissions and principals. Use roles to keep direct principal management small and predictable.
data.value: Roles bundle permissions and principals. Use roles to keep direct principal management small and predictable.

data.name: No roles have been created yet.
data.value: No roles have been created yet.

data.name: Describe the capability in business terms, not implementation details.
data.value: Describe the capability in business terms, not implementation details.

data.name: Delete this permission? Role links will also be removed.
data.value: Delete this permission? Role links will also be removed.

data.name: Deleting a permission removes it from all roles.
data.value: Deleting a permission removes it from all roles.

data.name: Use a stable key with letters, digits, dash, underscore or dot.
data.value: Use a stable key with letters, digits, dash, underscore or dot.

data.name: The app could not be saved.
data.value: The app could not be saved.

data.name: The app could not be deleted.
data.value: The app could not be deleted.

data.name: An app with the same key already exists in the selected module.
data.value: An app with the same key already exists in the selected module.

data.name: Delete or update dependent rows first.
data.value: Delete or update dependent rows first.

data.name: Public URL must be an absolute URL.
data.value: Public URL must be an absolute URL.

data.name: Selected module instance was not found.
data.value: Selected module instance was not found.

data.name: The selected app does not belong to the selected module instance's module.
data.value: The selected app does not belong to the selected module instance's module.

data.name: The selected host belongs to a different instance than the selected module instance.
data.value: The selected host belongs to a different instance than the selected module instance.

data.name: The selected artifact does not belong to the selected app.
data.value: The selected artifact does not belong to the selected app.

data.name: The app instance could not be saved.
data.value: The app instance could not be saved.

data.name: The app instance could not be deleted.
data.value: The app instance could not be deleted.

data.name: An app instance with the same key already exists in the selected module instance.
data.value: An app instance with the same key already exists in the selected module instance.

data.name: The artifact could not be saved.
data.value: The artifact could not be saved.

data.name: The artifact could not be deleted.
data.value: The artifact could not be deleted.

data.name: Use a stable host key with letters, digits, dash, underscore or dot.
data.value: Use a stable host key with letters, digits, dash, underscore or dot.

data.name: Base URL must be an absolute http or https URL.
data.value: Base URL must be an absolute http or https URL.

data.name: Base URL must not contain query string or fragment.
data.value: Base URL must not contain query string or fragment.

data.name: Base URL should only contain protocol, host and optional port.
data.value: Base URL should only contain protocol, host and optional port.

data.name: A host with the same key already exists in the selected instance.
data.value: A host with the same key already exists in the selected instance.

data.name: The host could not be saved.
data.value: The host could not be saved.

data.name: The host could not be deleted.
data.value: The host could not be deleted.

data.name: A row with the same key already exists.
data.value: A row with the same key already exists.

data.name: The instance could not be saved.
data.value: The instance could not be saved.

data.name: The instance could not be deleted.
data.value: The instance could not be deleted.

data.name: Schema names must start with a letter and then use letters, digits or underscore.
data.value: Schema names must start with a letter and then use letters, digits or underscore.

data.name: A module with the same key already exists.
data.value: A module with the same key already exists.

data.name: The module could not be saved.
data.value: The module could not be saved.

data.name: The module could not be deleted.
data.value: The module could not be deleted.

data.name: A module instance with the same key already exists in the selected instance.
data.value: A module instance with the same key already exists in the selected instance.

data.name: The module instance could not be saved.
data.value: The module instance could not be saved.

data.name: The module instance could not be deleted.
data.value: The module instance could not be deleted.

data.name: Use letters, digits, dash, underscore or dot. Keep permission names stable.
data.value: Use letters, digits, dash, underscore or dot. Keep permission names stable.

data.name: A permission with the same name already exists.
data.value: A permission with the same name already exists.

data.name: The permission could not be saved.
data.value: The permission could not be saved.

data.name: The permission could not be deleted.
data.value: The permission could not be deleted.

data.name: Use letters, digits, dash, underscore or dot. Keep the role name stable.
data.value: Use letters, digits, dash, underscore or dot. Keep the role name stable.

data.name: A role with the same name already exists.
data.value: A role with the same name already exists.

data.name: The role could not be saved.
data.value: The role could not be saved.

data.name: Select a principal type and enter a principal value.
data.value: Select a principal type and enter a principal value.

data.name: The role could not be deleted.
data.value: The role could not be deleted.

data.name: Allowed characters: letters, digits, dot, dash and underscore.
data.value: Allowed characters: letters, digits, dot, dash and underscore.

data.name: Topology detail pages for InstanceTemplateHosts, InstanceTemplateModuleInstances and InstanceTemplateAppInstances.
data.value: Topology detail pages for InstanceTemplateHosts, InstanceTemplateModuleInstances and InstanceTemplateAppInstances.

data.name: Use a stable role name, for example
data.value: Use a stable role name, for example

data.name: Artifacts are deployable outputs of an app definition. Keep the artifact metadata precise so app instances can reference the right build.
data.value: Artifacts are deployable outputs of an app definition. Keep the artifact metadata precise so app instances can reference the right build.

data.name: Use a known package type when possible so later deployment automation can behave predictably.
data.value: Use a known package type when possible so later deployment automation can behave predictably.

data.name: Deployment records track rollout requests and execution status. They are kept outside the main manual topology workflow.
data.value: Deployment records track rollout requests and execution status. They are kept outside the main manual topology workflow.

data.name: Permissions are stable capability names used by roles and apps. Prefer precise names that do not require frequent renaming.
data.value: Permissions are stable capability names used by roles and apps. Prefer precise names that do not require frequent renaming.

data.name: Roles are the main RBAC editing surface. Add permissions and principals here so access remains grouped and auditable.
data.value: Roles are the main RBAC editing surface. Add permissions and principals here so access remains grouped and auditable.

data.name: These permissions are granted whenever the role is resolved for a user or service identity.
data.value: These permissions are granted whenever the role is resolved for a user or service identity.

data.name: These pages are separated from the main admin workflow because they belong to template-driven rollout, deployment planning and future HostAgent automation. For manual setup, start with instances, hosts, modules, apps and artifacts.
data.value: These pages are separated from the main admin workflow because they belong to template-driven rollout, deployment planning and future HostAgent automation. For manual setup, start with instances, hosts, modules, apps and artifacts.

data.name: Assignments connect hosts to deployment planning. This is intentionally separated from the main manual admin workflow.
data.value: Assignments connect hosts to deployment planning. This is intentionally separated from the main manual admin workflow.

data.name: HostDeploymentAssignments.Lede
data.value: Assignments connect hosts to deployment planning. This is intentionally separated from the main manual admin workflow.

data.name: HostDeployments.Lede
data.value: Deployment records track rollout requests and execution status. They are kept outside the main manual topology workflow.
